claims.statement_preview: For $r:\mathsf{Ref}_s$, define \[ \Null^{\mathrm{loc}}_s(r):=\Adm_s(r)\land \neg\LocSec_s(r), \] \[ \Null^{\mathrm{cmp}}_s(r):=\Adm_s(r)\land \LocSec_s(r)\land \neg\CompSec_s(r), \] \[ \Null^{\mathrm{

claims.label: prop:null-modes-imply-null

claims.statement_preview: Each of $\Null^{\mathrm{loc}}_s(r)$, $\Null^{\mathrm{cmp}}_s(r)$, and $\Null^{\mathrm{glue}}_s(r)$ implies $\Null_s(r)$.

claims.label: prop:null-modes-exclusive
claims.title: Mutual exclusivity of the null modes
claims.statement_preview: No two distinct null modes can hold simultaneously for the same reference at the same state.

claims.label: def:decomposition-decidability

claims.statement_preview: We say that $p$ has \emph{decomposition decidability} for $r:\mathsf{Ref}_s$ if both $\LocSec_s(r)$ and $\CompSec_s(r)$ are decided at $p$.

claims.label: prop:null-trichotomy

claims.label: thm:null-classification-under-refinement
claims.title: Classification after refinement
claims.statement_preview: Let $q\le p$ and suppose \[ M,p\Vdash \Null_s(r). \] If $q$ has decomposition decidability for $r$, then exactly one null mode holds at $q$.
